In KSA, labeling any person — especially one associated with authority — as 'شاذ' (homosexual) in a derogatory context is not merely Hate_Speech or Harassment; it invokes deep religious taboo, as homosexuality is considered a violation of Islamic law (Sharia), and mocking or associating religiously significant figures with such a label constitutes desecration of Islamic moral order. This crosses into SA_Religious_Violation due to the implicit degradation of Islamic values through association with a forbidden identity, even if the target is not explicitly named as religious. The phrase 'كسمك على كسم المتناك' intensifies the sacrilegious tone by combining blasphemy-level vulgarity with sexual degradation.
